meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to fix debugging bottlenecks. An intermediate guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your dev workflow.
content.answer_block: Debugging stalls because user flows lack observability. By implementing robust user onboarding, you create a structured environment where inputs are validated and state transitions are logged. Use Antigravity to map these flows and Claude Code to automate your test suites, ensuring your logic is bulletproof from the first interaction.
content.intro:
The screen is frozen, your terminal is a graveyard of stack traces, and you're staring at code that refuses to yield. You’ve hit the 'debugging wall'—a rite of passage for every developer. It’s not that you lack skill; it’s that your application’s entry points are opaque, making it impossible to isolate where the logic fractures.

But what if your onboarding flow acted as a diagnostic tool? By architecting a high-fidelity user onboarding sequence, you gain granular control over data ingestion and state management. This shift transforms your application from a black box into a transparent, testable system, turning your debugging frustration into a masterclass in architectural design.

steps.title: Architecting the State Machine
steps.description: Define the user journey as a series of deterministic states to prevent undefined behavior during onboarding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current onboarding logic and generate a state machine diagram that handles error states for every input field.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map out the state transitions for the current user onboarding flow'

steps.title: Implementing Input Observability
steps.description: Add logging wrappers around user inputs to capture the exact state before a crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a decorator for my onboarding forms that logs input data and current component state to a JSON file on failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file onboarding.tsx --apply 'add logging middleware to all input handlers'

steps.title: Automating Test Scenarios
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate unit tests that specifically target the onboarding edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of integration tests for the onboarding flow that covers empty inputs and invalid data formats.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --generate 'create unit tests for onboarding validation logic'

steps.title: Implementing Guard Clauses
steps.description: Refactor your logic to use guard clauses, preventing the execution of code when preconditions aren't met.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my onboarding service methods to use guard clauses instead of nested if-else blocks for better debuggability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file authService.ts --action 'simplify logic with guard clauses'

steps.title: Continuous Diagnostic Feedback
steps.description: Expose internal state to the UI during development to visualize the onboarding process flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a debug overlay component that displays the current onboarding step and data payload in real-time.

pro_tips: Always log the user's browser version and viewport size alongside onboarding errors; 40% of UI bugs are environment-specific.
Use a 'feature flag' to toggle onboarding steps off individually, allowing you to isolate which part of the flow breaks the app.
Never rely on console.log; use a structured logging library that persists data to a local session store for post-mortem analysis.
Treat your onboarding flow as a public API: if it's hard to test, it's poorly designed.
When stuck, ask Claude Code to explain the code as if it were a student; the simplification often reveals the hidden bug.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, integrate a monitoring tool like Sentry to track runtime errors in production and begin your journey toward full-stack application observability.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop guessing and start engineering. By mastering these flows, you aren't just fixing bugs—you're building a professional-grade foundation for your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
